children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Administrator may award grants to directly eligible tribes under .section 605(b) of this titlesection 605 of this title

children.heading: Tribal applications
children.label: b Tribal applications
children.text: A directly eligible tribe may apply for a grant under by submitting an application to the Administrator that includes, as appropriate, the information required for an application by a State under .section 605 of this titlesection 605(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To ensure consistency with any applicable State homeland security plan, a directly eligible tribe applying for a grant under shall provide a copy of its application to each State within which any part of the tribe is located for review before the tribe submits such application to the Department.section 605 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: notify the Administrator, in writing, of that fact; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide an explanation of the reason for not supporting the application.

children.heading: Final authority
children.label: d Final authority
children.text: The Administrator shall have final authority to approve any application of a directly eligible tribe. The Administrator shall notify each State within the boundaries of which any part of a directly eligible tribe is located of the approval of an application by the tribe.

children.heading: Prioritization
children.label: e Prioritization
children.text: The Administrator shall allocate funds to directly eligible tribes in accordance with the factors applicable to allocating funds among States under .section 608 of this title

children.heading: Distribution of awards to directly eligible tribes
children.label: f Distribution of awards to directly eligible tribes
children.text: If the Administrator awards funds to a directly eligible tribe under this section, the Administrator shall distribute the grant funds directly to the tribe and not through any State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In allocating funds under this section, the Administrator shall ensure that, for each fiscal year, directly eligible tribes collectively receive, from the funds appropriated for the State Homeland Security Grant Program established under , not less than an amount equal to 0.1 percent of the total funds appropriated for grants under sections 604 and 605 of this title.section 605 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: receives fewer than 5 applications under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: does not approve at least 2 applications under this section.

children.heading: Tribal liaison
children.label: h Tribal liaison
children.text: A directly eligible tribe applying for a grant under shall designate an individual to serve as a tribal liaison with the Department and other Federal, State, local, and regional government officials concerning preventing, preparing for, protecting against, and responding to acts of terrorism.section 605 of this title

children.heading: Eligibility for other funds
children.label: i Eligibility for other funds
children.text: A directly eligible tribe that receives a grant under may receive funds for other purposes under a grant from the State or States within the boundaries of which any part of such tribe is located and from any high-risk urban area of which it is a part, consistent with the homeland security plan of the State or high-risk urban area.section 605 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: States shall be responsible for allocating grant funds received under to tribal governments in order to help those tribal communities achieve target capabilities not achieved through grants to directly eligible tribes.section 605 of this title

children.heading: Distribution of grant funds
children.label: 2 Distribution of grant funds
children.text: With respect to a grant to a State under , an Indian tribe shall be eligible for funding directly from that State, and shall not be required to seek funding from any local government.section 605 of this title

children.heading: Imposition of requirements
children.label: 3 Imposition of requirements
children.text: A State may not impose unreasonable or unduly burdensome requirements on an Indian tribe as a condition of providing the Indian tribe with grant funds or resources under .section 605 of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: k Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to affect the authority of an Indian tribe that receives funds under this part.
